title: elymus notius
intro: Elymus notius, commonly known as the Foothill Wildrye, is a perennial grass native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially aiding in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis and digestive issues. The therapeutic actions of Elymus notius are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, among others. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential therapeutic applications and bioactive constituents.
side_effects: Elymus notius can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach cramps due to its high content of toxic compounds. Overconsumption of the plant may also lead to kidney damage and impaired liver function. Additionally, improper use of Elymus notius can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems in some individuals. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can result in seizures, coma, and even death.
